channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: ACECQA Incident Response Template strengthens child safe governance expectations across ECEC
item.description: ACECQA's incident Response Template can support early childhood education and care (ECEC) providers to respond to, document and review incidents in a structured, child-centred and nationally consistent way.
item.content:encoded: ACECQA's incident Response Template can support early childhood education and care (ECEC) providers to respond to, document and review incidents in a structured, child-centred and nationally consistent way.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: TAFE NSW expands leadership capability investment through new program for emerging ECEC leaders
item.description: TAFE NSW is deepening its investment in leadership capability across the early childhood education and care (ECEC) sector, launching a targeted professional learning initiative designed to strengthen the skills and confidence of emerging leaders working in NSW services.
item.content:encoded: TAFE NSW is deepening its investment in leadership capability across the early childhood education and care (ECEC) sector, launching a targeted professional learning initiative designed to strengthen the skills and confidence of emerging leaders working in NSW services.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Institutional investors reassess their exposure to ECEC: what ART’s exit from G8 Education signals for the Sector
item.description: When a major institutional investor adjusts its position in an early childhood education and care (ECEC) provider, it rarely makes headlines. Yet these movements often reveal more about the sector’s financial health, investor sentiment, and long‑term confidence than any quarterly earnings call. The recent forms lodged by Australian Retirement Trust (ART), one of Australia’s largest superannuation funds, is one such moment worth paying attention to.
item.content:encoded: When a major institutional investor adjusts its position in an early childhood education and care (ECEC) provider, it rarely makes headlines. Yet these movements often reveal more about the sector’s financial health, investor sentiment, and long‑term confidence than any quarterly earnings call. The recent forms lodged by Australian Retirement Trust (ART), one of Australia’s largest superannuation funds, is one such moment worth paying attention to.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: The Y NSW welcomes Inquiry recommendation backing child safety
item.description: As a proud not-for-profit provider of outside school hours care (OSHC), the Y NSW has welcomed a key recommendation from a NSW Upper House Committee inquiry report on the Early Childhood Education and Care Sector, saying it reinforces the need to prioritise child safety in OSHC.
item.content:encoded: As a proud not-for-profit provider of outside school hours care (OSHC), the Y NSW has welcomed a key recommendation from a NSW Upper House Committee inquiry report on the Early Childhood Education and Care Sector, saying it reinforces the need to prioritise child safety in OSHC.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: ASQA Tribunal decision affirms cancellation of former Gills College childcare qualifications
item.description: The integrity of early childhood education and care (ECEC) qualifications has come under renewed national focus following a ruling by the Administrative Review Tribunal, which has affirmed the Australian Skills Quality Authority’s (ASQA) decision to cancel childcare qualifications issued by former registered training organisation Gills College.
item.content:encoded: The integrity of early childhood education and care (ECEC) qualifications has come under renewed national focus following a ruling by the Administrative Review Tribunal, which has affirmed the Australian Skills Quality Authority’s (ASQA) decision to cancel childcare qualifications issued by former registered training organisation Gills College.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Bronwen FitzGerald, Deputy Secretary, Early Childhood Education Victoria, to open Workforce and Wellbeing Forum 2026
item.description: With the second edition of The Sector Connect+ ECEC Workforce and Wellbeing Forum fast approaching, the program continues to take shape. The event will be held in Melbourne on Friday, 5 June 2026, and organisers have now confirmed Bronwen FitzGerald, Deputy Secretary, Early Childhood Education in the Victorian Department of Education, as a key participant.
item.content:encoded: With the second edition of The Sector Connect+ ECEC Workforce and Wellbeing Forum fast approaching, the program continues to take shape. The event will be held in Melbourne on Friday, 5 June 2026, and organisers have now confirmed Bronwen FitzGerald, Deputy Secretary, Early Childhood Education in the Victorian Department of Education, as a key participant.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: NSW Office of the Children’s Guardian expands free child safe eLearning resources
item.description: The NSW Office of the Children’s Guardian (OCG) is continuing to strengthen child safe capability across the community sector through a growing suite of free online training and eLearning resources focused on child protection, safeguarding and organisational responsibility.
item.content:encoded: The NSW Office of the Children’s Guardian (OCG) is continuing to strengthen child safe capability across the community sector through a growing suite of free online training and eLearning resources focused on child protection, safeguarding and organisational responsibility.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: From Country to Classroom: SNAICC Launches Culturally Grounded Learning Tools
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children has launched a unique culturally informed early Childhood Education and Care resource for early childhood workers. The Bushland Animal Yoga Cards’ offer ACCO and non-Indigenous ECEC services a resource that incorporates movement with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages, with native Australian animals representing various poses and movements.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children has launched a unique culturally informed early Childhood Education and Care resource for early childhood workers. The Bushland Animal Yoga Cards’ offer ACCO and non-Indigenous ECEC services a resource that incorporates movement with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages, with native Australian animals representing various poses and movements.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby Donations Update
item.description: SNAICC sincerely thanks everyone who took the time to contribute and donate towards Kumanjayi Little Baby's family over recent weeks.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC sincerely thanks everyone who took the time to contribute and donate towards Kumanjayi Little Baby's family over recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 22 May 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to the NT child protection inquiry, proposed changes to the Child Placement Principle, and BBC coverage of Kumanjayi Little Baby.
item.content:encoded: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to the NT child protection inquiry, proposed changes to the Child Placement Principle, and BBC coverage of Kumanjayi Little Baby.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: To improve children’s mental health, start by supporting their parents
item.description: Long-term data shows children are more likely to develop mental health issues when their parents experience financial stress and housing instability.
item.content:encoded: Long-term data shows children are more likely to develop mental health issues when their parents experience financial stress and housing instability.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Leadership Calls for Real Action on Child Safety
item.description: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander leaders call for real action on child safety; warn NT law changes undermine Child Placement Principle.
item.content:encoded: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander leaders call for real action on child safety; warn NT law changes undermine Child Placement Principle.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC Raises Alarm Over Narrow NT Child Protection Inquiry Scope
item.description: SNAICC says narrow scope of NT Child Protection Inquiry fail to address systemic issues needed to keep Aboriginal children and families safe.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC says narrow scope of NT Child Protection Inquiry fail to address systemic issues needed to keep Aboriginal children and families safe.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Is baby talk bad? Why ‘parentese’ actually helps babies learn language
item.description: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.content:encoded: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 15 May 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, the Federal Budget was a missed opportunity for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities. NT Government has introduced legislation that undermine the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Child Placement Principle. And a heartwarming Mother's Day story from a member of SNAICC's Youth Voice advisory group on how love, kinship and culture can help children in out-of-home-care grow up Deadly.
item.content:encoded: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, the Federal Budget was a missed opportunity for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities. NT Government has introduced legislation that undermine the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Child Placement Principle. And a heartwarming Mother's Day story from a member of SNAICC's Youth Voice advisory group on how love, kinship and culture can help children in out-of-home-care grow up Deadly.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Budget Falls Short for Aboriginal Children Despite Community-Led Solutions on the Table
item.description: Budget 2026 missed opportunity for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children and families with community-led solutions on the table.

item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Sector Peak Urges NT Government to Scrap Legislative Changes and Partner on Child Protection
item.description: SNAICC is urging the NT Government to abandon proposed child protection legislative changes that would weaken the Child Placement Principle.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC is urging the NT Government to abandon proposed child protection legislative changes that would weaken the Child Placement Principle.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 1 May 2026
item.description: This is not the news we wanted to hear. This week has been a tragedy that has shattered the nation and devastated the community of Mparntwe/Alice Springs.
item.content:encoded: This is not the news we wanted to hear. This week has been a tragedy that has shattered the nation and devastated the community of Mparntwe/Alice Springs.
